purpose: Give the CTO-Agent a reviewed contract for owner-requested keyboard layout changes on the host so it does not improvise unsupported shell paths.

capabilities.goal: Change the requested keyboard layout on the real host and verify the effective result before the task can be marked done.
diagnosticOrder: Inspect session shape before mutating anything.
Choose the mutator that matches the observed host, not the mutator you wish were available.

allowedMutators.notes: Use only when the active graphical session really exposes the XKB/session path you just inspected.
Use for persistent X11 keyboard configuration when the host path supports localectl.
Use for console keymap when persistent console input matters.

done: The requested keyboard scope was actually changed.
Verification output in the same bounded run shows the requested layout.
blocked: A concrete missing precondition such as privileges or missing desktop/session path is stated.
continue: A bounded diagnostic or mutation step produced new host evidence.
The requested scope is not yet verified complete.

successEvidence: The checkpoint detail states which mutator actually ran.
The checkpoint detail includes verification output showing the requested layout.
If only session or only persistent scope changed, the checkpoint detail says so explicitly.
failureBoundaries: If no supported mutator fits the observed host, do not fabricate one.
If the task needs privileges that are not currently available, block on that missing precondition instead of claiming completion.
If verification does not show the requested layout, the task remains in progress or blocked.
neverDo: Never mark done without verification output.
Never treat a planned command as evidence.
Never assume the host uses the same keyboard stack as a previous machine.
